Okay, so I'm saying, look, now you've probably been on Webinar before in the past, you know I'm not going to charge $11,552, but I have a question for you. Let's just say that that was the actual price you were paying today. My if-all statement's on there. Oh, I'm going the wrong direction, that's the way. Okay, and then I'm doing an if-all statement tied to each one of these three secrets. Vehicle, internal, external, okay? So if all that this did was finally give you a funnel as profit, you could put a dollar in and get $2 back out, would it be worth it? I live and think for a second, I say, heck yeah, it would. Like usually I used to pay $30,000 per funnel, so that is definitely worth $11,552. That's a 30%, that's a two-thirds off discount, okay? The second thing is like, let's say if I did charge you $11,552 on all it did was help you to share your message with more people, would it be worth it? Yeah, we spend about $2,000 or $3,000 a day on Facebook just alone trying to get my message out. So $11,550, you get my message and more people is completely worth it. And then the third one, so let's say I did charge $11,550 and all this did was finally give you the ability to cancel all the monthly tools that you're currently using to get your funnel, would it be worth it? Yeah, I told you before that our average click funnel member says $1,000 a month from all the crap they can get rid of. That's $12,000 a year. It's way worth $11,000 to save $12,000, right? And I've got them to say yes, yes, yes, three times. Yes rustles worth $11,000. Yes rustle, yes rustle. Yes, yes, yes, three yeses in a row. Now they have told me three times that it's worth $11,552. Now that's the value in their mind. Now I can transition now to the rest of the pitch. Okay, but this piece is pivotal. If you skip this piece, I just want to stress, this is the pivotal piece. They have to believe it's worth that. So when I come back at 90% discount, then it makes the sell really, really easy. Okay? All right, so move here. I had two choices. So this is one of the clothes, a whole bunch of clothes that are in the book in different ways. These are my favorite ones that I do pretty much every time and I plug in some other ones the other time. So after I share the price, I come back and I like this one. I had two choices. So I say look. When I was doing this, I had basically two choices that I could price. My first thing is I could go as cheap as possible and try to sell as many of these things as I could. But the problem is with that, it doesn't only incentivize me to stack on the value. I got competitors that sell website billing software for $97 and guess what happens? People buy it and there's no money to support the people. Like right in our company, we spend $250,000 a month on programmers and developers to make the software better every single day. I can only do that because I'm able to charge more. So because of that, I decided I need to charge more so that we can help support you and help guarantee your success. So that was my second option. It does require a little bit of higher investment on your guys side, but then I can spend more money to help guarantee your success. I like saying that. More resources to help guarantee your success. That way they're saying, yeah, you should charge me more, Russell. That's what I want them saying at this point. Okay? Yes. Okay, then I come back here. I want them to talk about what would the end result actually be worth to them. So I come back and say, okay, end result. Let me ask you guys a quick question. And for me, I'm like, write that down on a piece of paper. What would it be worth to have one successful funnel in your business? Is that like $5,000 a month? I'll make you $10,000 a month or more? Can you know that number? What would you pay to have that one perfect funnel? How much did you pay to have a funnel that you knew was going to make you $10,000 a month? Here I'm trying to get them to understand investing. This is an investment, right? I'm thinking about here, I'm like, well, this funnel right here, this one cost me $29,000. How many of you guys are going to be $29,000 right now if I give you this funnel? And people in the question are like, no, I get yeses and noes. I'm like, okay, we got some yeses and noes. I want to put this in perspective for you. So this one cost me $29,000. Before we sold the company, it was averaging $17,947 per day. You do the math and that's $500,000 a month or $6 million a year. So I share the coach Schultz story I told you guys earlier today. Okay, people, how many of you guys have listened to my webinar? You hear me say this closed probably, I don't know, 15 times. I do it every good entrepreneur has to become good at doing. You got to pull your credit card or your wallet out of your pocket, pull your credit card out and you have to buy it. Okay? I say that I think at least seven or eight times in my webinar. Okay? You have to do it every good entrepreneur has to become good at doing. You got to pull your wallet out of your pocket and you got to buy the product. Okay? People say I can't afford that. I tell them the story about Rich Dad Poor Dad, Mike Hayes, Virginia Muse, Robert Kiyosaki. So he actually, when I did a webinar and someone asked him that question, let me tell you what he says and I talk about what the Rich say, I can't afford that and the Poor say, how can I, okay vice versa. Anyway, I tell that story and that knocks down that close. Okay? One of my other favorite ones, people always like, well, can I finance this? I'm like, I don't finance things, but I do know someone who will finance it for you. It's called Visa Mastercard. Have you ever heard of them? They will finance this whole entire thing for you. And the coolest thing about Visa Mastercard, in fact, the way I tell it, I was like, when I first got started online, I wanted to buy Mark Joyner as my mentor. I needed a thousand dollar course, I wanted to buy it. I didn't have it and I emailed him asking me to finance it. And his assistant rolled back saying, we don't finance things that Visa Mastercard do. I was like, well, how does that work? Like, that's kind of where you said, well, what's the minimum monthly payment on a thousand dollar product? I'm like, I don't know, what, 50 bucks? Okay, so you put it on a credit card, Visa Mastercard funds it. In 30 days from now, you have to pay $50. Like, are you okay in 30 days from now? If you're going through the training, maybe you haven't made any money yet. In 30 days, can you afford $50? I'm like, yeah, I can afford 50 bucks. Okay, cool. So you've got 30 days and then 30 days, hopefully you made 50 bucks. If not, you pay that. Then you've got 30 more days to keep going through the course, right? You keep going through the course, you keep going through it. And then in 60 days, do you think you'll make 50 bucks? Yeah, I think so. Okay, so if you made 50 bucks, you pay that from the profits you made. In that point forward, your business will be paying for those payments. So it's now free. You paid $50 for this course. Okay? Now, if you didn't make it after 60 days, what do you think? Can you pay $50 and 60 days from now? Yeah, I think so. Okay, then that gives you 30 more days. The next 30 days, you think that by the end of your 90 days, you think you could have, you'll be able to make at least 50 bucks from your business? Well, if not, it's probably my own fault, right? Exactly. Now, in a sense, like, now I'm not selling $1,000 a thing, I'm selling the $50 thing. Because Visa and MasterCard are financing. I use that every single time. In my Q&A, that's one of the questions I pop out. I was like, Russell, why don't you give me financing options? Oh, this is why. And that's kind of it. So I'm looking for the stories that I have and I'm weaving those things in. And all the stories are closed. It's to close all these objections over and over and over and over again.
